findings.description: apply_run_rewards() never appends a run entry to meta['run_history']. The array is always empty. No historical run data is ever persisted (biome, score, anam, death, cards_played, timestamp).
findings.bible_ref: bible s.5.1 — echo_memory, run_history for cross-run context

findings.description: Even once run_history is populated, no cap enforced. Director confirmed: 50 derniers runs max. Without cap, file grows unbounded after hundreds of runs.

load_profile() clamps faction_rep only for keys already present. If a faction is missing (e.g. save from older version), it is not added during clamping loop. _migrate() adds missing factions but only if version < 1.0.0.
load_run_state() clamps life_essence but NOT anam_accumulated. A corrupted/tampered save could set anam_accumulated to negative or extremely high value. Should clamp to >= 0.
load_profile() performs full disk I/O on each call (no in-memory caching). Correct per director q-20260414-003.
Backup (.bak) created before every write. load_profile() falls back to .bak if primary corrupted.
_validate_run_state() checks required keys, numeric types, life_max >= 1, cards_played >= 0.
Legacy slot 1-3 and autosave migration functional via _try_migrate_from_legacy().
_validate() + fallback to _get_default_profile() on corruption. run_state discarded if validation fails.
Single profile (PROFILE_PATH = user://merlin_profile.json) + run_state co-located. Correct bible s.13.4 schema.
version != CURRENT_VERSION triggers migration. If save is from a future version (downgraded client), migration would corrupt data. Should also check version > CURRENT_VERSION as a guard.
int(Time.get_unix_time_from_system()) cast to int — safe until year 2038 on 32-bit. GDScript int is 64-bit so OK, but worth noting.

invariants_verified: load_profile() always reads from disk (no cache)
